config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan “kepentingan masyarakat”, karena itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas untuk menekan kelompok yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dijaga, bukan dikesampingkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse by majorities, governments, or institutions. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only when those limits are necessary, narrow, and accountable. If we flip the default and put society first, it becomes too easy to trample free speech, due process, and minority rights in the name of “the greater good.” A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals cheaply; it is one that protects rights while solving shared problems.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya juga tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau keputusan yang menguntungkan kelompok mayoritas sementara orang kecil tidak punya daya tawar. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau ketertiban umum yang menuntut pembatasan sementara, tetapi itu harus ketat, transparan, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang lahir bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang gampang sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, “the common good” has too often been the language used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the people being asked to sacrifice are the least powerful. That said, I do think there are limited cases like public health emergencies, natural disasters, or immediate threats to safety where narrow, temporary restrictions can be justified if they are transparent and accountable. The key difference is that society should not erase individual rights by default; it should earn any exception and keep it tightly constrained.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi pembenaran untuk menekan yang lemah, dari pembatasan kebebasan sampai keputusan yang lebih menguntungkan mayoritas atau elite. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau ancaman kesehatan publik, tetapi pembatasannya harus sementara, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap menyelesaikan kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. In the United States, I have seen how “the common good” can become a convenient excuse for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the people paying the price are the least powerful. I do think narrow exceptions are justified in real emergencies, like public health crises or immediate safety threats, but those exceptions have to be temporary, proportionate, and closely accountable. If a society can only function by routinely overriding individual rights, then it is solving problems the wrong way.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
